message:
feat: Add storage layer for config, cache, and token accounts

src/storage/mod.rs - Module Organization:
- Re-exports: paths, config, cache, token_accounts
- Unified storage interface for all persistence needs

src/storage/paths.rs - Platform-Specific Paths (~2.6KB):

XDG-compliant directory resolution using directories crate:

macOS:
- Config: ~/Library/Application Support/caut/
- Cache: ~/Library/Caches/caut/
- Data: ~/Library/Application Support/caut/

Linux:
- Config: ~/.config/caut/
- Cache: ~/.cache/caut/
- Data: ~/.local/share/caut/

Windows:
- Config: %APPDATA%\caut\
- Cache: %APPDATA%\caut\cache\
- Data: %APPDATA%\caut\data\

Key functions:
- config_dir(): Main configuration directory
- cache_dir(): Temporary/cached data
- data_dir(): Persistent user data
- config_file_path(): Path to config.toml
- token_accounts_path(): Path to token-accounts.json
- ensure_dirs(): Create directories if missing

src/storage/config.rs - Configuration File (~44KB):

TOML-based configuration at ~/.config/caut/config.toml:
```toml
default_provider = "both"

[output]
format = "human"
color = true

[providers]
codex = true
claude = true
gemini = false

[web]
timeout_seconds = 30
```

Config struct with serde:
- default_provider: ProviderSelection
- output: OutputConfig (format, color, pretty)
- providers: HashMap<String, bool> enable/disable
- web: WebConfig (timeout, retry settings)

Key functions:
- load(): Read and parse config file
- load_or_default(): Fallback to defaults if missing
- save(): Write config to disk
- merge_with_args(): Override config with CLI flags

src/storage/cache.rs - Cache Layer (~1.2KB):

File-based caching for cost scan results:
- Cache directory: ~/.cache/caut/cost-usage/
- Per-provider cache files: codex-v1.json, claude-v1.json
- TTL-based invalidation (configurable)

Key functions:
- get(): Retrieve cached entry if valid
- set(): Store entry with timestamp
- invalidate(): Force cache miss
- is_stale(): Check TTL expiration

Cache entry format:
```json
{
  "payload": { ... },
  "cached_at": "2026-01-18T12:00:00Z",
  "ttl_seconds": 300
}
```

src/storage/token_accounts.rs - Multi-Account Storage (~4.6KB):

JSON-based account storage for OAuth tokens:
```json
{
  "version": 1,
  "providers": {
    "claude": {
      "version": 1,
      "accounts": [
        {
          "id": "uuid-here",
          "label": "personal",
          "token": "sk-ant-...",
          "addedAt": "2026-01-18T...",
          "lastUsed": null
        }
      ],
      "activeIndex": 0
    }
  }
}
```

TokenAccounts struct:
- version: Schema version for migrations
- providers: HashMap<String, ProviderAccounts>

ProviderAccounts:
- accounts: Vec<TokenAccount>
- active_index: Currently selected account

TokenAccount:
- id: UUID for unique identification
- label: Human-friendly name ("work", "personal")
- token: OAuth token (sensitive)
- added_at: Creation timestamp
- last_used: Optional last access time

Key functions:
- load(): Read token-accounts.json
- save(): Write with atomic rename
- get_active_account(): Current account for provider
- set_active_account(): Switch active account
- add_account(): Register new account
- remove_account(): Delete by label or index
- list_accounts(): Enumerate all for display

Security considerations:
- Tokens stored in plaintext (user's config dir)
- File permissions: 600 (owner read/write only)
- Consider keyring integration for sensitive tokens
- No cloud sync by design (local-only)

Design principles:
- Fail gracefully if files missing (use defaults)
- Atomic writes to prevent corruption
- Schema versioning for future migrations
- Cross-platform path handling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
